lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hello, young adventurer. What brings you to the Museum's paleontology floor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's paleontology?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I'm glad you asked! Paleontology is the study of prehistoric lifeforms in Gielinor through the examination of fossils.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: We use fossils to try determine several things: information about what fossil belonged to, what the environment was like, and try to uncover secrets of Gielinor's past.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: What else would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do these fossils come from?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Ah, brilliant question. Fossils of creatures are found in sedimentary rock, we use specialised tools to carefully remove the unwanted rock only leaving only the fossil behind.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: If you're looking for fossils yourself, I hear there is a voyage to a mysterious island to discover new types of fossil.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: You should speak to the Curator as he knows more about it than I.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do with these fossils?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Any fossils you find can be added to the surrounding display cases.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: If you manage to complete display case, the Museum will ensure you are rewarded for your efforts.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Also, if you find any rare fossils on your travels, make sure you hold onto them. Something tells me they will be very valuable in the future...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Do you have any other questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about some of the fossil displays?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: It looks like the Museum doesn't have any complete fossil displays yet. I could only speculate on what they will contain.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Of course. Which display would you like to ask about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a great leviathan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a foot print of a mythical animal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like an imprint of a posterior.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a zygomite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a snake in the shape of a hoop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a clam shell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a 'big' foot print.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a crab shell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a cyclopse with two heads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like it could be used for fuel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a tar creature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a giant dragonfly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like an ancient cave painting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a person with a fish tail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a lava creature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a pufferfish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a dragon skull.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a squirrel with huge teeth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a dragon egg.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a cross between a mushroom and kangaroo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like an unusual lobster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a creature with a giant skull and small body.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a tree sized mushroom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like scary mushrooms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a tortoise with a mushroom on its shell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a huge pitcher plant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a cross between a dark beast and a shark.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a merfish with legs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a plant with a stack of mushrooms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a big mushroom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a toucan with two heads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one that looks like a bird with a snail shell on its back.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Um... yes. It's an interesting specimen isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know much about this display?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Um... yes, but I couldn't possibly share that information right now.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: It's an interesting specimen isn't it? It appears that the specimen is thousands of years old!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well that's what the analysis from the fossil shows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you going to tell me about the display?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: What did you ask about again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nevermind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nevermind.
